title: Observation of Switchable Chiral Magnons in an Altermagnet
authors: Zheyuan Liu, Hodaka Kikuchi, Zijun Wei, Shinichiro Asai, Mechthild Enderle, Ursula B. Hansen, Vasile O. Garlea, Manh D. Le, Gøran J. Nilsen, Igor A. Zaliznyak, Takatsugu Masuda
abstract: Chiral magnons, the quanta of handed spin waves, transport spin angular momentum without energy loss due to Joule heating. The recently discovered altermagnets were proposed to host chiral magnons arising from a non-relativistic exchange mechanism, similar to that in ferromagnets but without net magnetization, offering a stray-field-free platform for efficient magnon spin-current manipulation. In this work, we directly observed chiral magnons in the altermagnetic prototype MnTe using polarized inelastic neutron scattering. Furthermore, the magnon chirality was found to be reversibly switched by magnetic-field control, establishing a robust foundation for functional altermagnetic magnonics.
summary: This paper provides the first direct experimental observation of switchable chiral magnons in the altermagnet MnTe. By using polarized neutron scattering, the authors show that the handedness of spin waves can be controlled by magnetic field cooling, providing a foundation for future altermagnetic spintronic devices.
main_problem: The researchers aimed to experimentally verify the existence of chiral magnons in altermagnets, which are a predicted but previously unobserved hallmark of the altermagnetic state arising from non-relativistic exchange.
main_result: The study directly observed chiral magnons in the altermagnet MnTe and demonstrated that their chirality can be reversibly switched using a magnetic-field cooling process.
method: The team used polarized inelastic neutron scattering (PINS) on the HYSPEC and IN20 spectrometers, combined with polarized neutron diffraction (PND) to control and measure domain imbalance.
model_or_system: The physical system is MnTe, a prototypical altermagnet with a collinear magnetic structure and $g$-wave spin symmetry, featuring two opposite-spin sublattices.
key_observables: Chiral magnon signal (Mch), symmetric structure factor (S(Q, E)), magnon band splitting, and the Néel vector component (n_x).
important_parameters: Magnetic-field cooling (FC) through the Neel temperature (TN = 307 K) using fields of approximately +/- 4.2 mT.
main_assumptions: The observed chirality is attributed to an imbalanced multi-domain state rather than a weak ferromagnetic moment.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the crystal and magnetic structure; Fig 2 displays 1D cuts of the neutron structure factor showing split magnon peaks and opposite chirality; Fig 3 details the experimental setup and the sign reversal of the chiral signal after field cooling.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the concept of altermagnetic chiral magnons, describes the experimental setup and MnTe crystal properties, presents the observation of magnon splitting and chirality via neutron scattering, demonstrates the switchability of chirality via field cooling, and concludes by ruling out alternative ferromagnetic origins.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Left: Crystal and magnetic structure of MnTe. Right: Classical schematic of chiral magnon modes in an altermagnet. The two nondegenerate modes, Ω1 and Ω2, possess right- and left-handed chirality, respectively. (b) Left: Antiphase magnetic domains corresponding to the left panel of (a). Right: The chiralities of the corresponding magnon modes, opposite to those in the right panel of (a). (c) Calculated neutron chiral term Mch for the magnon modes Ω1 and Ω2 with the domain state in (a). The gray curve indicates the Q−E coverage in the HYSPEC experiment.
